framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Public signal-integrity / EMI mastery through teaching
paths.what_it_is: Build deep, rigorous expertise in signal integrity, EMI, and PCB noise control, and codify it into a publicly accessible course, textbook, or conference talk other engineers rely on.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves your understanding is precise enough to teach and defend in public, not just apply privately - the rarest and most trusted form of technical credibility.

paths.what_it_is: Open up hardware - chips, instruments, vintage and modern devices - and publish a detailed, rigorous write-up of how the internals actually work.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can read and explain real circuits from the ground up, a form of hands-on technical fluency that is hard to fake.

paths.path: Open-hardware project leadership with public PCB documentation
paths.what_it_is: Lead an open-hardware project and publish the schematics, PCB layouts, and design-decision rationale for others to study and build on.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can carry a design from concept to a manufacturable, well-documented product, and are confident enough in the work to expose it to public scrutiny.
